tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" -i -f "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest" </dev/null

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "new" ]]; then
  echo "-f after -i did not overwrite" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -f overwrites.
tests.expected: Destination contains the source content after -i -f.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

printf 'n\n' | "$UTIL" -f -i "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "-i after -f did not preserve on no" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -i prompts before overwrite.
tests.expected: Answering no to the final interactive prompt leaves the destination unchanged.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" -f -n "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "-n after -f clobbered destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -n does not overwrite an existing file.
tests.expected: Destination remains unchanged under -f -n.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" -n -f "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest" </dev/null

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "new" ]]; then
  echo "-f after -n did not overwrite" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -f overrides -n.
tests.expected: Destination is overwritten under -n -f.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

mkdir "$tmpdir/target"
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"

"$UTIL" -t "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/src"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/target/src")"
if [[ "$actual" != "payload" ]]; then
  echo "-t did not move source into directory" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY moves all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY.
tests.expected: The source file appears inside the target directory with its content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
mkdir "$tmpdir/destdir"

set +e
"$UTIL" -T "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/destdir" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "-T unexpectedly accepted directory destination" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -T, --no-target-directory treats DEST as a normal file; moving a file onto an existing directory as that file is an error.
tests.expected: The command exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

mkdir "$tmpdir/target"
printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"

set +e
"$UTIL" -T -t "$tmpdir/target" "$tmpdir/src" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "-T and -t were both accepted" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -T no-target-directory and -t target-directory are mutually exclusive destination-selection modes.
tests.expected: The command exits nonzero.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --update=none "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "--update=none replaced existing file" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=none is like --no-clobber: no destination files are replaced and skipped files do not induce failure.
tests.expected: Existing destination content is preserved.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

set +e
"$UTIL" --update=none-fail "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest" >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "--update=none-fail did not fail on skipped file" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=none-fail skips existing destinations but diagnoses skipped files and induces a failure.
tests.expected: The command exits nonzero when the destination already exists.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"
touch -d '2024-01-02 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/src"
touch -d '2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --update=older "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "new" ]]; then
  echo "--update=older did not replace older dest" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=older replaces an existing destination file if it is older than the corresponding source file.
tests.expected: Older destination is replaced with source content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"
touch -d '2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/src"
touch -d '2024-01-02 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --update=older "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "--update=older replaced newer dest" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=older does not replace an existing destination file that is newer than the source file.
tests.expected: Newer destination content is preserved.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"
touch -d '2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/src"
touch -d '2024-01-02 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --update "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "bare --update did not default to older" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update with no UPDATE argument defaults to older, not all.
tests.expected: Bare --update preserves a destination newer than the source.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"
touch -d '2024-01-01 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/src"
touch -d '2024-01-02 00:00:00 UTC' "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --update=all "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "new" ]]; then
  echo "--update=all did not replace newer dest" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --update=all replaces all existing files in the destination; this is also the default operation when --update is not specified.
tests.expected: Destination is replaced even though it is newer than the source.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --backup=simple "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest~")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "simple backup did not contain old dest" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup=simple makes a simple backup of each existing destination file using the usual '~' suffix.
tests.expected: The simple backup file contains the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --backup=numbered "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest.~1~")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "numbered backup did not contain old dest" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup=numbered makes numbered backups.
tests.expected: The first numbered backup dest.~1~ contains the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'prior\n' > "$tmpdir/dest.~1~"
printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --backup=existing "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest.~2~")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "existing backup did not choose numbered" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup=existing makes numbered backups if numbered backups already exist, simple otherwise.
tests.expected: With dest.~1~ already present, the new backup is dest.~2~ containing the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --backup=none "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

if [[ -e "$tmpdir/dest~" ]]; then
  echo "--backup=none created a backup" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --backup=none, also named off, never makes backups even if --backup is given.
tests.expected: No simple backup file is created.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

"$UTIL" --backup=simple --suffix=.bak "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest.bak")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "--suffix did not override backup suffix" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: -S, --suffix=SUFFIX overrides the usual backup suffix.
tests.expected: The backup uses .bak and contains the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

VERSION_CONTROL=numbered "$UTIL" --backup "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest.~1~")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "VERSION_CONTROL did not select numbered" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: The version control method may be selected through the VERSION_CONTROL environment variable when --backup is used.
tests.expected: VERSION_CONTROL=numbered creates dest.~1~ containing the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'new\n' > "$tmpdir/src"
printf 'old\n' > "$tmpdir/dest"

SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX=.save "$UTIL" --backup=simple "$tmpdir/src" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest.save")"
if [[ "$actual" != "old" ]]; then
  echo "SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX was not used" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: The backup suffix is '~' unless set with --suffix or SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX.
tests.expected: The simple backup uses the .save suffix and contains the old destination content.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'payload\n' > "$tmpdir/src"

"$UTIL" --strip-trailing-slashes "$tmpdir/src/" "$tmpdir/dest"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/dest")"
if [[ "$actual" != "payload" ]]; then
  echo "trailing slash was not stripped from source" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes removes any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument.
tests.expected: A file source named with a trailing slash is moved after stripping the slash.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

printf 'left\n' > "$tmpdir/a"
printf 'right\n' > "$tmpdir/b"

"$UTIL" --exchange "$tmpdir/a" "$tmpdir/b"

actual="$(<"$tmpdir/a")"
if [[ "$actual" != "right" ]]; then
  echo "--exchange did not swap files" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --exchange exchanges source and destination.
tests.expected: After exchange, the original destination content is at the source path.

tests.body:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT

set +e
"$UTIL" --suffix >/dev/null 2>&1
status=$?
set -e

if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
  echo "--suffix without argument succeeded" >&2
  exit 1
fi
tests.exercises: --suffix=SUFFIX has a mandatory argument; absent SUFFIX is an option error.
tests.expected: The command exits nonzero when --suffix is missing its argument.
